raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Assembly passed Assembly Bill (AB) 1474, requiring prosecutors and judges to consider economic costs associated with lengthy criminal sentences.",
    "AB 1474 mandates that prosecutors state the estimated cost of the sentence they recommend during sentencing.",
    "Judges are required to state the estimated cost of the sentence imposed under AB 1474.",
    "Estimates of sentencing costs will be based on standardized calculations provided by the Department of Finance and the Board of State and Community Corrections.",
    "California leads the nation in spending on corrections, which detracts from funding for other public policy priorities.",
    "California's incarcerated population has declined by nearly one-quarter over the past decade due to various policy changes.",
    "The population on parole in California has declined by nearly one-half in the same period.",
    "Despite a decline in the incarcerated population, California's spending on corrections has increased by over $3 billion since 2010-11.",
    "California spends more on corrections than Texas and New York combined.",
    "A 2019 report by the California State Auditor indicated that the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation had done little to verify the performance of its programs.",
    "Prosecutors and judges historically have not considered the financial costs of incarceration in their sentencing decisions.",
    "Prosecutors in Pennsylvania and Virginia currently state the estimated cost to taxpayers for the sentences they recommend.",
    "Legal scholar Richard Posner has advocated for judges to consider the costs of imprisonment among other factors in criminal sentencing.",
    "AB 1474 aims to enhance transparency in sentencing by focusing on the economic and opportunity costs of incarceration.",
    "The implementation of cost transparency in sentencing may lead to a reassessment of the effectiveness of investments in incarceration."
  ]
}
```
